notes: HERMIT 12-card gravity deck. The lighthouse at 2:47 AM working on Estelle: the small absences that aren't dramatic but accumulate — radio silence, the lens's quiet rotation, the bunk room widening. Effects bias toward LONELINESS gain unless Estelle LOGS or TENDS LAMP this turn.

cards.title: THE LENS TURNS ALONE
cards.flavor: It has been turning since six PM. It will turn until dawn. You hear it twelve feet above your head — the brass on brass.

then.text: You tended it on the hour. It turns alone with your help.

cards.title: THE RADIO IS DEAD AIR
cards.flavor: You key the mic at 0247. You release the key. The hiss fills the room. The hiss fills the room.

cards.title: THE BAYOU DOES WHAT THE BAYOU DOES
cards.flavor: A gator coughs at the south pylons. A heron lifts. The water settles.

effects.text: Heron up off the third pylon. Marker noise in the log.

cards.title: YOU REMEMBER THE PREDECESSOR
cards.flavor: Captain Eaves, '52 to '72. He retired three months before you started. Died in '79. His coat is still on the peg.

cards.title: THE LOGBOOK IS OPEN TO YOU
cards.flavor: Page 412 of the current volume. The pen is uncapped. The line below 0214 LAMP STEADY NO TRAFFIC is waiting.

then.text: 0301 LAMP STEADY NO TRAFFIC. Written. The page registers.

cards.title: A SKIFF PASSES IN THE DISTANCE
cards.flavor: Outboard, low rpm. South-east, two hundred yards out. Jules's running lights — green on starboard.

cards.title: THE BUNK ROOM WIDENS
cards.flavor: The single bunk is the single bunk. The wall behind it appears to have moved back a foot. You measure with your eye and the wall is the wall again.

cards.title: THE LAMP COULD BE TENDED LATER
cards.flavor: The thought, briefly. You name the thought. You write it on the corner of the log. You cross it out.

options.label: Tend the lamp now (cost: 1 Time; gain: −2 LONELINESS, lamp_tended_count +1)

cards.title: THE RADIO SPEAKS
cards.flavor: A voice through the static. Channel sixteen. "Bayou Light, this is Coast Guard New Orleans, radio check."

cards.title: THE LENS HESITATES
cards.flavor: On the third quadrant. Not stopping. A half-beat of slow. It happens once a watch this time of year.

effects.text: Lens hesitated, Q3. Note it in the log.

cards.title: YOUR HAND REMEMBERS THE CHART
cards.flavor: Your hand goes to the chart drawer without your thinking. You unroll the chart. You haven't looked at it in years. You haven't needed to.

cards.title: DAWN IS TWO HOURS AWAY
cards.flavor: By 0430 the lens will catch the edge of east. By 0500 the sky over the bayou will start. By 0530 the watch is over and the relief — when there is relief — comes.

options.label: Climb to the lens (cost: 2 Time; gain: −2 LONELINESS, lamp_tended_count +1, reveal lens_hatch threshold)

effects.text: You go up. The lens stage smells of brass polish and ozone.
